channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: iOS 18.1 will fix a bug that made it harder to free up iPhone storage
item.description: With many-megapixel cameras and the ability to shoot video at up to 4K, an iPhone’s storage fills up quickly. Sure, ballooning apps and digital downloads like audiobooks and movies also take up space, but they are easy to offload and reload as needed.
item.dc:creator: Sean Captain, Fast Company

item.title: AI is now a crucial tool in Trump’s image-management arsenal
item.description: An image of Donald Trump cosplaying as a football player last weekend set the tone for the final push of the Republican nominee’s campaign. It depicts Trump as a yoked, veiny-arm baller glowering from within a Steelers uniform on the field at Pittsburgh’s Acrisure Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: 5 lesser-known iOS 18.2 tricks that are coming to your iPhone – and how try them
item.description: The iOS 18.2 developer beta is full of interesting new features for your iPhone. Here are a few you might have missed.
item.dc:creator: alexblake.techradar@gmail.com (Alex Blake), TechRadar

item.title: Russia amplified hurricane disinformation to drive Americans apart, researchers find
item.description: New research shows Russia has worked to amplify false and misleading claims about the U.S. government's response to Hurricanes Helene and Milton as part of the Kremlin's efforts to manipulate Americans before the Nov. 5 election.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Many UK workers still aren't using AI at work
item.description: Workers are lacking the right tools and clear guidance for AI implementation despite promises of more investment.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How to check your heartbeat with ECG app on your Apple Watch
item.description: The ECG app on Apple Watch Series 4 or later and all models of Apple Watch Ultra generates an ECG similar to a single-lead (or Lead I) ECG.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia's Website Survives Cyberattack: Sustained Foreign Efforts to Undermine US Elections
item.description: Cybersecurity threats, foreign influence operations, and election meddling: a comprehensive look at recent events in the United States
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Gartner IT Symposium/Xpo 2024: Top Takeaways on Artificial Intelligence
item.description: Gartner IT Symposium/Xpo 2024 key takeaways, challenges, and future directions in artificial intelligence for CIOs and IT leaders.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Set up your smart speaker for emergencies
item.description: While you may already use your smart speaker for playing music, telling you the weather and answering trivia questions, there are also commands you can tell your device to get help in an emergency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why enterprises should care more about net neutrality
item.description: Net neutrality is a topic that arouses nearly as many people as politics does, in no small part because it’s been a political issue. But nearly all of that emotion is at the consumer level. CIOs and network operations and planning professionals cite the topic as important in less than 5% of cases.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The power of prime numbers in computing
item.description: Everyone knows that a prime number is one that cannot be made by multiplying other whole numbers together. This deceptively simple attribute hides a world of complexity that has engaged mathematicians ancient and modern.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: ABBA, Radiohead and The Cure musicians sign AI protest letter against 'unlicensed use' of works
item.description: Musicians from ABBA, Radiohead and The Cure have joined actors and authors in signing a protest letter against the mining of their artistry to build artificial intelligence tools.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New middleware doubles GPU computational efficiency for AI workloads in trials, says Fujitsu
item.description: New middleware from Fujitsu has achieved more than a 2x increase in GPU computational efficiency for artificial intelligence (AI) workloads in trials according to the company, which designed the technology specifically to help solve the issue of GPU limitations and shortages related to computing dem
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Big offshore wind project proposed for New York as other sites are evaluated in 3 states
item.description: BRIGANTINE, N.J. (AP) — Offshore wind energy projects in New York, New Jersey and Maryland are moving forward, as federal regulators examine the proposals and opponents escalate their legal challenges to the work.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The iPad mini is still the best small tablet around
item.description: We waited three years for Apple to update the iPad mini, and what we got is more of the same — boosted with a much better processor. Apple’s iPad mini has consistently been positioned as either the most versatile, or the most underpowered tablet in the company’s lineup.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Intel, AMD forge x86 alliance
item.description: The old adage “the enemy of my enemy is my ally” has been borne out again, with AMD and Intel joining forces with a number of their largest partners to create the x86 Ecosystem Advisory Group, a consortium that aims to drive forward the ecosystem around x86 processors.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Vertiv and Nvidia define liquid cooling reference architecture
item.description: Nvidia has partnered with hardware infrastructure vendor Vertiv to provide liquid cooling designs for future data centers designed to be AI factories. AI factories are specified data centers emphasizing AI applications as opposed to traditional line of business applications like databases and ERP.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: GoFundMe bets social media can unlock Gen Z giving. Meta partnership, new tools will test that
item.description: “We play a really important role helping people ask for help and give help in the world,” GoFundMe CEO Tim Cadogan said.
item.dc:creator: JAMES POLLARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AI stagnation: The gap between AI investment and AI adoption
item.description: AI continues to shape cloud strategies, but AI implementation is going slower than most predicted. This is frustrating for technology providers who have made big bets on AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Alabama man was arrested Thursday for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said. Eric Council Jr.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: HPE, Dell launch another round of AI servers
item.description: The AI spin continues, with Hewlett Packard Enterprise and Dell Technologies both introducing new servers oriented towards training large language models (LLM).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Rep. Tlaib calls out Kroger for facial recognition practices
item.description: Kroger said it uses facial recognition to present targeted ads on its digital price tags that it rolled out five years ago.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Kernstine, NewsNation
